title: Software Engineer 5 - TV & Web Player Platform
location: Los Gatos,California,United States of America
description: At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Come be a part of what’s next. The TeamThe TV & Web Player Platform team is responsible for developing streaming video players that deliver industry-leading playback experiences within the Netflix app on consumer electronic devices and web browsers. Our players operate on millions of devices, including Smart TVs, streaming sticks, game consoles, set-top boxes, and browsers like Chrome, Firefox, and Edge. We aim to delight our members with innovative playback features and exceptional streaming quality.Our  team is at the forefront of testing a diverse set of innovative activities related to product feature expansion. This includes creating tests for playback features such as Live Streaming, Seamless Ads playback, and Ident innovation. We conduct A/B tests to thoroughly evaluate features and ensure they perform optimally without regressions. We are deeply involved in continuous integration and release management testing of our video player, which is core to our operations. This critical function ensures the delivery of the best streaming experience to our members by maintaining high standards of quality and performance. Our team collaborates with various cross-functional teams across Netflix, including UI, Encoding, Streaming Algorithms, Security, and Open Connect, to support product development and experimentation through rigorous testing.This  team consists of engineers from diverse backgrounds, bringing expertise across multiple disciplines, including embedded systems, streaming media playback, content protection, client-server communication, networking protocols, continuous integration, test automation, experimentation, and software engineering. Our success is driven by self-motivated, collaborative, and curious engineers who prioritize the team's goals.The RoleWe are seeking a senior software engineer with deep expertise in test development, automation, CI/CD pipelines, and build and release processes to join our team. Your primary responsibilities will include designing and implementing complex automated tests and strategies that ensure the highest quality streaming experiences on both TV and Web devices. You will lead efforts to optimize and automate our build, integration, and release workflows, ensuring rapid and reliable delivery of new features and fixes. We are the creative collective behind Netflix’s most high-profile brand initiatives, developing work that builds passionate fandoms and shapes how the world connects with our brand. Our mission is to strengthen the Netflix brand and drive business value by creating culturally relevant marketing that is as entertaining as our shows, films, games, and experiences. Our studio serves as a centralized partner for a wide range of business-critical areas—spanning Global Brand and Brand Partnerships, Ads, Consumer Products, and live experiences like Netflix House. As Netflix continues to expand into new business units, formats, and physical experiences, the volume and complexity of design output across the company continues to grow. To support this scale, BCS is evolving its operating model to include a dedicated production design team focused on speed, systems, and consistency, without compromising craft.THE ROLEWe are seeking a Production Designer to join the Brand Creative Studio. This role is an individual contributor within the production design team, responsible for delivering high-quality, brand-led design output at scale across multiple business functions, including the Netflix Brand, Netflix House, Product Marketing, Consumer Products, and Corporate Communications.This role reflects a creative agency and design studio model of production design, where designers work within established systems to execute a high volume of work efficiently and thoughtfully. You will partner closely with the Senior Manager, Production Design and cross-functional teams to bring brand strategy and design systems to life across channels, markets, and formats.A strong portfolio demonstrating solid design fundamentals, attention to detail, and experience working within brand systems is required.RESPONSIBILITIESProduction Design & ExecutionDesign and produce high-volume, brand-led assets across a range of outputs, including lower-funnel marketing assets, campaign toolkits, sales materials, and presentation design.
 Execute work within established brand systems, templates, and production frameworks, ensuring consistency and quality across deliverables.
 Apply strong design fundamentals to every output, with attention to typography, layout, hierarchy, and clarity.Cross-Functional Creative SupportCollaborate with partners across Brand, Product Marketing, Consumer Products, Netflix House, and Corporate Comms to support a wide range of creative needs.
 Contribute to presentation design for executive, sales, and high-priority communications where clarity and brand expression are critical.
 Ensure work is fit-for-purpose across audiences and channels while adhering to Netflix brand standards.Systems & Workflow ContributionSupport the ongoing development and refinement of templates, modular systems, and production workflows.
 Adapt existing systems to new formats, channels, and use cases as needed.
 Work efficiently within fast-moving timelines while maintaining a high standard of craft.Craft, Quality & GrowthUphold quality and attention to detail across all production outputs.
 Incorporate feedback thoughtfully and iterate quickly.
 Stay current on best practices in production design, brand systems, and design tooling.
 QUALIFICATIONS4–6 years of experience in design within a creative agency, design studio, or in-house brand team.
 Strong foundation in visual design, including typography, layout, hierarchy, and composition.
 Experience working within established brand systems and templates.
 Ability to execute high-volume design work efficiently without sacrificing quality.
 Exposure to a range of outputs, such as marketing assets, toolkits, sales materials, and presentation design.
 Strong collaboration skills and comfort working with cross-functional partners.
 Fluency in modern design tools and workflows (Figma, Adobe Creative Suite; motion experience a plus).
 Clear communicator who is receptive to feedback and eager to learn.
 Highly organized and comfortable working in fast-paced environments.
 Interest in growing within a production design or systems-led design practice. Generally, our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. We use a number of Netflix investments and innovations to power our ads - a unique mix of client and server-side ad insertions, state-of-the-art content delivery systems, ad encoding recipes, content understanding and metadata, etc. We deliver ads in a manner that’s thoughtful of our member’s viewing experience and drive great outcomes for advertisers. We also ensure that advertiser brand safety is ensured during serving, members only see the most appropriate ads for them.The Media Planning team is at the heart of designing and delivering state-of-the-art media planning solutions that power Netflix’s advertising business. We are developing innovative, in-house ad tech systems that enable advertisers and internal partners to efficiently plan, allocate, and optimize media investments across Netflix’s platform. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next.About Market ActivationMarket Activation (MA) ensures our commercial teams are fully prepared to bring Ads products to market with clarity, speed, and impact. We set the standard for go-to-market readiness and launch execution by:Driving structured, repeatable launch executionTranslating product capabilities into clear seller workflows, enablement, and GTM strategyActing as the connective tissue across Product, Sales, and Operations to ensure aligned, high-quality commercializationThe RoleWe are hiring a Program Manager to lead Commercial Readiness for the Media Planning & Activation product domain, one of the most critical operational pillars of our Ads business.This role sits at the center of product, and sales,and is responsible for ensuring that new capabilities are operationalized, adopted, and scaled effectively across global markets.Responsibilities Own end-to-end commercial readiness and launch execution for Media Planning & Order Management productsEffectively execute the change management of new capabilities by collaborating with multiple teams across the organization including Sales, Account Management, Market Planning & Operations, Product, and other cross-functional teams. Drive change management, including documentation, communication, and enablement inputsDrive cross-functional alignment on commercialization strategy and end-user positioning.Lead structured forums to surface risks, unblock decisions, and maintain execution momentumTranslate product capabilities into clear, scalable operational workflowsCoordinate UAT and validate end-to-end workflows to ensure systems and processes are production-readyEstablish feedback loops for business users and Product to surface friction, inefficiencies, and product gapsPartner with Enablement to align on right-sized training and rollout strategies About you:8+ years of experience in the advertising industry, including 5+ years leading complex, cross-functional programs; deep expertise in the CTV and streaming ecosystemProven track record of delivering complex initiatives in dynamic, highly cross-functional environmentsExperience leading launches of advertising business tools (e.g., media planning, order management, billing systems), including operational readiness, communications, positioning, workflow design. Highly organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environmentDemonstrated ability to navigate ambiguity and drive execution across diverse stakeholder groupsStrong relationship builder with the ability to influence and operate as a trusted partner in business transformationAdaptable and resilient, with a proven ability to thrive in evolving, high-growth environments
  Generally, our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. You choose each year how much of your compensation you want in salary versus stock options. To determine your personal top of market compensation, we rely on market indicators and consider your specific job family, background, skills, and experience to determine your compensation in the market range. The range for this role is $200,000.00 - $330,000.00.Netflix provides comprehensive benefits including Health Plans, Mental Health support, a 401(k) Retirement Plan with employer match, Stock Option Program, Disability Programs, Health Savings and Flexible Spending Accounts, Family-forming benefits, and Life and Serious Injury Benefits. We also offer paid leave of absence programs. Full-time hourly employees accrue 35 days annually for paid time off to be used for vacation, holidays, and sick paid time off. Full-time salaried employees are immediately entitled to flexible time off. See more details about our Benefits here. Netflix is a unique culture and environment. Learn more here.Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner.We are an equal-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.

title: Product Manager, Content Aggregation Experiences
location: Los Gatos,California,United States of America
description: At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next.As we grow globally, we have increasing opportunities to communicate with and delight our members and prospective members in new ways. One of the primary levers for growth is developing new plans, with differentiated features and price points, so consumers can choose what is right for them. This position is part of the broader Commerce Product team. In this role, you will be responsible for leading the design and development of content experiences that drive engagement, grow the business, and entertain the world! This role is based in our Los Gatos office (preferred) in a hybrid model, but we are open to remote candidates who are on the West Coast (PST timezone). Travel to Los Gatos will be required if remote-based.As a Product Manager, you will be expected to design, strategize, execute, and prioritize projects. You should be comfortable at the hub of a fast-paced environment where you interface daily with cross-functional experts. You can build consensus and alignment but are not afraid to push ahead when there remain some critics and lead in the face of ambiguity.  What will you do:Product Execution: Lead the execution and optimization of content aggregation roadmaps. Take product concepts from ideation through validation, shipping, measurement, and iteration.Test and Iterate: Drive rigorous experimentation to evaluate new content formats, refine content discovery, and maximize incremental member engagement and satisfaction. Build members experiences that drive engagement and measure the impact of your product innovation, with your design, research, and data science partners.Scale the Platform: Build and scale product experiences and platform capabilities to support the onboarding of content partners globally. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with cross-functional leaders in articulating a compelling product vision, and then breaking the vision into well-scoped projects and driving execution. Work as part of an autonomous agile team along with designers, engineers, consumer insights, and data science, ML & analytics colleagues to deliver outstanding products to our customers.Advocate and Align: Ensure that the broader organization is clear about our product strategy and roadmap, and effectively farm for dissent. Deeply understand and socialize the evolving needs of our customers, with your consumer insights and design partners.Culture: Foster an inclusive environment, where all viewpoints are welcomed and encouraged. Embodies the Netflix real values while also bringing a new perspective to improve our culture.Who are we looking forPassion for Design, Product Excellence, Growth, Business Model Innovation, and/or Behavioral Economics!  Past experience in entertainment industry or related products/solutions is a huge plus.7+ years of product management experience with a proven record of prioritizing and successfully launching impactful products or platforms.Worked in a direct-to-consumer business, preferably with a global scope, where the consumer is paying for the service.Demonstrated leadership working effectively with designers, data scientists, engineers, researchers, and colleagues across content, communications, and marketing - ability to inspire, motivate and lead a cross-functional teamVery high attention to detail, with an ability to connect the smallest experience elements to the big picture goals at the company and team level.Excellent verbal and written communication skills - An ability to synthesize the complex into the simple and persuasive, and tell compelling stories with data.Comfortable using data to inform priorities and evaluate impact of initiativesHas strong instincts for outstanding customer experience.Experience working in an organizational structure with both vertical and horizontal teams where ownership of a capability cuts across multiple teams.Embodies the Netflix real values while also bringing a new perspective to improve our culture. We strongly believe that diversity of experiences, perspectives, and backgrounds will lead to a better environment for our employees and better products for our customers.  Generally, our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. You choose each year how much of your compensation you want in salary versus stock options. To determine your personal top of market compensation, we rely on market indicators and consider your specific job family, background, skills, and experience to determine your compensation in the market range. The range for this role is $310,000.00 - $545,000.00.Netflix provides comprehensive benefits including Health Plans, Mental Health support, a 401(k) Retirement Plan with employer match, Stock Option Program, Disability Programs, Health Savings and Flexible Spending Accounts, Family-forming benefits, and Life and Serious Injury Benefits. We also offer paid leave of absence programs. Full-time hourly employees accrue 35 days annually for paid time off to be used for vacation, holidays, and sick paid time off. Full-time salaried employees are immediately entitled to flexible time off. See more details about our Benefits here. Netflix is a unique culture and environment.
